mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a tightening labor market that reduces the supply of labor, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces the availability of goods, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.8 million signals a strengthening demand for housing that increases the availability of credit, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 3-month increase in the CPI above 2.5% signal a monetary easing cycle that increases inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial jobless claims above 400,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces the supply of labor, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 4% signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that increases the availability of credit, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the real stock of inventories signals a weakening supply chain that reduces the availability of goods, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in 70% of triggered instances.
